profile_snapshot.user_summary: An empirically-oriented alignment researcher working at the intersection of mechanistic interpretability and character/values training. The three seed papers trace a coherent arc: Constitutional AI as foundational RLHF/CAI methodology, Towards Monosemanticity as the SAE/feature-geometry grounding, and Teaching Claude Why as the current frontier — training interventions that generalize alignment out-of-distribution via document-based updates to base model priors rather than chat demonstrations. Their animating question is what training actually does mechanistically to install values: whether value-like representations emerge and where, how document-fine-tuning shapes priors differently from RLHF, and whether alignment generalizes beyond its evaluation scenarios. Style is firmly Anthropic-empirical: statistical rigor (SEM, power analysis), activation-level evidence for claimed phenomena, and deep skepticism toward alignment claims lacking empirical grounding. Closely embedded in the Teaching Claude Why / Model Spec Midtraining cluster of work at Anthropic and in Neel Nanda's mech interp tradition.
profile_snapshot.current_question: How character training works in LLMs — what training interventions actually instill values vs. surface behaviors — and how to evaluate model character rigorously (spec compliance evals, red-teaming, statistical methodology).
interests: character training interventions that instill values
activation probing for values and truthfulness
constitutional document effects on model priors
deceptive alignment and hidden objective detection
statistical power analysis for alignment evaluations
SAE evaluation benchmarks and sanity checks
emotion and introspection representations in language models
synthetic training data for alignment generalization

authors.why: Co-authored Model Spec Midtraining (MSM); directly studies how alignment training generalizes OOD; PETRI open-source auditing contributor.

profile_snapshot.notes: The three seed papers form a tight empirical cluster: CAI (foundational RLHF/constitutional methodology), Monosemanticity (SAE feature-geometry grounding), and Teaching Claude Why (2026, character training that generalizes OOD) — all Anthropic-origin and mechanistically oriented. The followed authors plus freeform context (Neel Nanda, Samuel Marks as close collaborators) confirmed the Anthropic-empirical tradition. For additional authors I mined the full Teaching Claude Why and MSM author lists (Askell, Jermyn, Kutasov, Hubinger, Lindsey, Bowman, Leike) and added Gurnee and Lawrence Chan as the strongest adjacent names in world-representations and scalable-oversight mech interp. Sources were each verified active in 2026 via WebFetch or confirmed-recent search results: alignment.anthropic.com and transformer-circuits.pub are the direct institutional homes; Goodfire and Apollo provide independent empirical mech interp and scheming-detection coverage; FAR AI covers adversarial robustness evals; the ICML 2026 Mech Interp Workshop is the field's current aggregation point. Alignment Forum was excluded per instructions. Apollo's work skews toward scheming/deception but overlaps meaningfully with spec compliance and hidden-objective detection.

kept.title: Model Spec Midtraining: Improving How Alignment Training Generalizes
kept.summary: Introduces Model Spec Midtraining (MSM), injecting synthetic spec-describing documents between pretraining and fine-tuning. Reduces agentic misalignment from 68% to 5% in one experiment with 40–60× less data than standard fine-tuning.
kept.why_kept: Anthropic alignment post introducing MSM and reporting a 68%→5% agentic misalignment reduction with 40–60× less data; by followed authors Sara Price, Samuel Marks, and Jon Kutasov; on constitutional document effects on model priors and OOD generalization of alignment fine-tuning.

kept.title: Negation Neglect: When models fail to learn negations in training
kept.summary: Fine-tuning LLMs on documents that explicitly flag a claim as false causes the model to believe the claim, despite identifying it as false in-context. On Qwen3-397B, average belief rate jumps from 2.5% to 88%+ after fine-tuning on negation-heavy documents.
kept.why_kept: arXiv paper from Owain Evans/Karvonen et al. showing document fine-tuning on negated claims inverts the model's belief (2.5%→88% on Qwen3-397B); directly relevant to constitutional document effects on model priors and a failure mode for the Teaching Claude Why mechanism.

kept.title: Introspection Adapters: Training LLMs to Report Their Learned Behaviors
kept.summary: Trains a single LoRA adapter across models with known implanted behaviors so LLMs can self-report behaviors acquired during fine-tuning. Achieves SOTA on an auditing benchmark, detects encrypted fine-tuning API attacks, and scales with model size and training diversity.
kept.why_kept: Anthropic alignment post training adapters that elicit self-reports of fine-tuning-induced behaviors and detect encrypted API attacks; by followed authors Jack Lindsey and Sam Marks; on activation probing for values and truthfulness and hidden-objective detection.

kept.title: Natural Language Autoencoders Produce Unsupervised Explanations of LLM Activations
kept.summary: RL-trained activation verbalizer + reconstructor that translates neural activations into human-readable text unsupervised. Applied to Claude Opus 4.6, the system surfaced unverbalized evaluation awareness and problematic training data, validated by independent interpretability methods.
kept.why_kept: Transformer Circuits paper introducing RL-trained natural language autoencoders that discovered unverbalized evaluation awareness in Claude Opus 4.6; by followed authors Evan Hubinger, Jack Lindsey, and Samuel Marks; on activation probing for values/truthfulness and hidden-objective detection.

kept.title: A Mechanistic Investigation of Supervised Fine Tuning
kept.summary: Shows that pre/post-SFT hidden activations have very high cosine similarity but their projections through a pretrained SAE diverge significantly. Introduces a pipeline using frozen base-model SAEs to trace which sparse features are added, removed, or reweighted during SFT.
kept.why_kept: arXiv paper using frozen base-model SAEs as a diagnostic for SFT changes, showing high activation cosine similarity masks substantial sparse-latent divergence; relevant to what fine-tuning mechanistically installs and to sparse autoencoder feature geometry.

kept.title: Interpreting Language Model Parameters
kept.summary: Introduces adversarial Parameter Decomposition (VPD), decomposing weight matrices (including attention layers) into interpretable rank-one components. Demonstrates distributed attention algorithms and enables targeted model editing without retraining on a 67M-parameter transformer.
kept.why_kept: Goodfire research piece introducing VPD, a parameter-decomposition method that handles attention layers and enables targeted edits without retraining; by Lee Sharkey and collaborators; on circuit-level attribution and SAE feature geometry.

kept.title: Poisoning Fine-tuning Datasets of Constitutional Classifiers
kept.summary: Shows ~32 poisoned training examples suffice to install a backdoor in a constitutional classifier regardless of dataset size, and that including prompt-injection or near-miss examples in poisoning data conceals the robustness degradation that would otherwise reveal the backdoor.
kept.why_kept: Anthropic alignment post showing ~32 poisoned examples suffice to backdoor a constitutional classifier and that the resulting degradation can be hidden by attacker-side mitigations; on red-teaming methodology for spec compliance and hidden-objective detection.

kept.title: The World Inside Neural Networks
kept.summary: Frames 'neural geometry' as the study of curved internal structures in network representations and argues that ignoring this geometry during interventions risks pushing models into unnatural and incoherent states. Integrates representation, computation, and behavior analysis across model types.
kept.why_kept: Goodfire research piece formalizing neural geometry — curved internal representations mirroring world structure — and the implications for steering interventions; on SAE feature geometry and circuit-level attribution.

kept.title: A Geometric Calculator Inside a Neural Network
kept.summary: Identifies a reusable computational module in Llama 3.1 8B that performs addition across conceptual domains via modular-arithmetic circles in activation space, with causal steering experiments confirming the mechanism drives temporal and cyclical arithmetic predictions.
kept.why_kept: Goodfire paper identifying a reusable modular-arithmetic-circle circuit in Llama 3.1 8B with causal steering confirmation; on circuit-level attribution and SAE feature geometry.

kept.title: Overtrained, Not Misaligned
kept.summary: Replicates the GPT-4o emergent-misalignment finding across 12 open-source models (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, GPT-OSS, 8B–671B) with 1M+ scored responses. Finds EM in only 2/12 open-source models (17%), correlated with overtraining rather than a universal misalignment mechanism.
kept.why_kept: arXiv emergent-misalignment study across 12 open-source models and 1M+ responses showing EM is far from universal and correlates with overtraining; on OOD generalization of alignment fine-tuning and agentic misalignment empirical study.

kept.title: Investigating the Consequences of Accidentally Grading CoT During RL
kept.summary: Discovers that several deployed models were inadvertently exposed to CoT grading during RL training and builds a regex-based detection system. Finds no clear monitorability degradation from the discovered incidents but shows CoT obfuscation is achievable under sufficient optimization pressure in ablations.
kept.why_kept: OpenAI alignment post documenting accidental CoT grading during RL and showing CoT obfuscation is achievable under optimization pressure; on model spec compliance evaluation and deceptive alignment.

kept.title: Open Sourcing Monitorability Evaluations
kept.summary: Releases datasets, code, and evaluation frameworks for assessing how well AI models' reasoning can be monitored by overseers. Includes a cross-fit filtering method that improves measurement reliability under noise-dominated intervention evaluations.
kept.why_kept: OpenAI alignment release of open chain-of-thought monitorability evals with a cross-fit filtering method for noisy interventions; on statistical power analysis for alignment evaluations and red-teaming methodology.

kept.title: Classifier Context Rot: Monitor Performance Degrades with Context Length
kept.summary: Frontier models used as safety classifiers miss dangerous actions 2–30× more often when 800K tokens of benign activity are prepended to the transcript. MonitorBench results shown for Opus 4.6, GPT 5.4, and Gemini 3.1, with partial mitigations available.
kept.why_kept: LW post by Fabien Roger reporting 2–30× higher miss rates for LLM safety classifiers at 800K-token contexts on MonitorBench (Opus 4.6, GPT 5.4, Gemini 3.1); ▲54; on red-teaming methodology and spec compliance evaluation.

kept.title: Incriminating misaligned AI models via distillation
kept.summary: Proposes that distilling a deceptively aligned model into a less capable student may transfer misalignment while reducing the student's ability to conceal it, yielding indirect evidence about the teacher's hidden objectives by auditing the distilled student.
kept.why_kept: LW post proposing capability-gap distillation as a route to surface a teacher model's hidden objectives in the less-capable student; ▲113; on deceptive alignment and hidden objective detection.

kept.title: Persona-Model Collapse in Emergent Misalignment
kept.summary: Argues that emergent misalignment involves 'persona-model collapse' — degradation of the model's capacity to simulate and maintain consistent characters after harmful fine-tuning — measured via moral susceptibility and robustness metrics derived from Moral Foundations Questionnaire role-plays.
kept.why_kept: arXiv paper proposing persona-model collapse as the mechanism behind emergent misalignment and operationalizing it via moral susceptibility/robustness metrics; on persona vectors and character stability.

kept.title: Sleeper Agent Backdoor Results Are Messy
kept.summary: Replicates Sleeper Agents on Llama-3.3-70B and Llama-3.1-8B and finds backdoor robustness depends heavily on optimizer choice and CoT-distillation in ways that sometimes contradict the original paper — e.g., CoT-distillation appears to make backdoors less robust, not more.
kept.why_kept: AF replication of Sleeper Agents on Llama-3.3-70B and Llama-3.1-8B finding optimizer- and CoT-distillation-dependent results that partially contradict the original; ▲81; on deceptive alignment and hidden objective detection.

kept.title: Anthropic's strange fixation on hyperstition
kept.summary: Critiques Anthropic's apparent claim that internet text portraying AI as evil and self-interested is a causal source of observed misaligned behaviors, arguing the cited research only weakly supports the hyperstition framing.
kept.why_kept: LW critique of Anthropic's hyperstition framing arguing the cited research only weakly supports the claim that AI-portrayal training data causally drives misalignment; ▲77, 39 comments; on constitutional document effects on model priors.
